title: Pair creation amplitudes for a real scalar field coupled to a time-dependent surface in d+1 dimensions
authors: C. D. Fosco, B. C. Guntsche
abstract: We study the pair creation phenomenon for a real scalar field $\varphi$ in the presence of a surface that undergoes time-dependent deformations, while imposing Dirichlet-like boundary conditions. Including terms up to fourth order in the departure of the surface from an infinite plane, we present results for the angular dependence of the emission rate for the vacuum-to-pair process as a function of the geometry and the dynamics of the surface, as well as of the momenta of the emitted pair. We check the consistency of the leading contribution with previous results obtained from the imaginary part of the effective action, and clarify how the relation between exclusive probabilities and the imaginary part of the effective action is modified at fourth order by the opening of a two-pair channel.
summary: This theoretical paper investigates pair creation from a scalar field interacting with a moving boundary. It advances the calculation to fourth order, demonstrating that while lower orders confirm standard quantum field theory relations (like the optical theorem), higher orders reveal complex non-linear physics, including new second-harmonic emission and vacuum depletion effects.
main_problem: The study calculates pair creation amplitudes for a real scalar field due to its interaction with a time-dependent, deforming surface in d+1 dimensions.
main_result: The calculation establishes that while the leading order probability correctly reproduces twice the imaginary part of the effective action (the optical theorem), higher orders reveal nonlinear effects, such as genuine second-harmonic emission and vacuum depletion corrections.
method: The authors employ perturbative quantum field theory techniques, expanding transition amplitudes in powers of the surface deformation parameter ($\psi$) and comparing results derived from exclusive scattering amplitudes with those obtained from the imaginary part of the effective action.
model_or_system: The system involves a massless real scalar field coupled to a time-dependent boundary defined by $\Sigma$. The interaction is modeled via an action term proportional to $\delta(x_d - \psi(\mathbf{x}_q))$, analyzed in the limit of Dirichlet boundary conditions.
key_observables: Pair creation probability density, radiated power per unit solid angle ($ rac{d\Omega}{Aq}  rac{dW}{dk_0}$), and the total decay probability $P^{(2)}$ and $P^{(4)}$.
important_parameters: The surface deformation function $\psi(\mathbf{x}_q)$, the coupling constant $\lambda$, and the dimensionality $d+1$. The analysis focuses on high-order corrections (up to fourth order).
main_assumptions: The calculation relies heavily on parity selection rules for non-vanishing terms, and several results are derived in the limit where the boundary condition is strictly Dirichlet ($\lambda 	o +\infty$).
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates an angular distribution ($\propto \cos	heta$) for a harmonically oscillating bump. Figure 2 compares the pair-creation rate $\gamma(\omega)$ vs. $\omega/K_s$ for varying coupling strengths, showing spectral departures. Figure 3 shows the cutting rule relating the discontinuity of the effective action to the two-particle phase-space integral.
paper_structure: The paper systematically calculates the amplitude expansion ($A = \sum A^{(n)}$) and the resulting probability density ($	ext{dP} \propto |A|^2$) up to fourth order. It then rigorously compares these direct calculations with established identities involving the imaginary part of the effective action, highlighting deviations at higher orders.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to open quantum systems and quantum optics because it provides a rigorous framework for calculating non-perturbative vacuum excitation processes (like the Dynamical Casimir Effect) beyond simple leading-order approximations, showing how nonlinearities manifest in multi-pair channels.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Angular distribution of the emitted particles in the localized, small- source regime, Eq. (41): a cos θ lobe along the normal to the surface. The dotted curve is the mirror image emitted into the xd < 0 half-space.

Figure 2: Pair-creation rate γ(ω) of Eq. (55) (in units of b2K2 s/(4π)) as a function of ω/Ks, for several values of λ/Ks. The Dirichlet limit (dashed) is the parabola 2(ω/Ks)(1 −ω/Ks). For λ ≲Ks the spectrum departs from the Dirichlet shape and develops a mild bimodal structure, the Lorentzian factor [1 + (2kq/λ)2]−1 favoring asymmetric partitions of the energy Ks.
